title: Property Tax Benefit for Disabled Veterans
summary: Reduces property taxes on an Idaho residence (and up to one acre) for qualifying disabled veterans.

requirements.notes: Benefit may reduce property taxes by up to $1,500. Apply through county assessor.

eligibilityNotes: Must be an Idaho resident, homeowner, and have a 100% service-connected disability. Surviving spouses may continue the benefit in limited circumstances per state rules.

title: Veteran Designation on Idaho Driver’s License / ID
summary: Adds a Veteran designation to an Idaho driver’s license or identification card.

requirements.notes: Must present acceptable proof of veteran status/honorable discharge (or other qualifying proof) at a driver’s license office.

eligibilityNotes: Available to veterans who can present acceptable documents showing honorable discharge (or other ITD-accepted proof).

title: Disabled Veteran License Plates
summary: Special Idaho plates for qualifying disabled veterans (application through ITD).

requirements.notes: Uses ITD application; documentation required (see form).

eligibilityNotes: Generally for honorably discharged veterans with 100% service-connected permanent disability; see ITD form instructions for required documentation.

title: Idaho State Parks Disabled Veteran Lifetime Pass
summary: Lifetime pass that waives day-use fees and provides camping fee waivers for eligible disabled veterans.

requirements.notes: Waives day-use for one personal vehicle with veteran present; waives usage fees for one campsite (reservation fees still apply).

eligibilityNotes: Eligibility commonly requires Idaho residency and 100% service-connected Permanent & Total disability (per program application).

title: Idaho Fish & Game Disabled Veteran (DAV) Reduced-Fee Licenses/Tags
summary: Reduced-fee DAV license and reduced-fee tags for eligible resident disabled veterans.

requirements.notes: DAV license and reduced-fee tags/permits available; specific documentation and eligibility rules apply.

eligibilityNotes: Program details, pricing, and required documentation are defined by Idaho Fish & Game on the DAV programs page.

title: Idaho State Veterans Homes (Boise, Lewiston and others)
summary: State veterans homes providing skilled nursing and supportive services for eligible veterans (and in some cases spouses).

requirements.notes: Admission criteria, medical need, and bed availability apply.

eligibilityNotes: Facilities and details are managed through Idaho Division of Veterans Services; check specific home pages for admissions packets and eligibility.

title: Idaho State Veterans Cemetery (Boise / Blackfoot) – Pre-Registration & Interment
summary: State veterans cemetery interment program; pre-registration available to verify eligibility in advance.

requirements.notes: Eligibility generally follows National Cemetery Administration rules plus Idaho law; pre-registration helps families.

eligibilityNotes: No Idaho residency requirement is stated for eligibility on the Idaho State Veterans Cemetery info pages; rules follow NCA eligibility and Idaho law.

title: Armed Forces & Public Safety Officer Dependent Scholarship (Tuition/Fee Waiver + Support)
summary: Tuition/fee waiver and additional support for eligible children/spouses of military members or public safety officers (Idaho program).

requirements.notes: Program has eligibility rules; includes tuition/fees waiver plus additional amounts for books and may include housing/subsistence per program rules.

eligibilityNotes: Program is described by Idaho Division of Veterans Services; eligibility and application details are handled through the linked program resources.

title: State Education Assistance Program (SEAP)
summary: Up to $8,000 per state fiscal year in tuition and fee reimbursement for Idaho National Guard members attending Idaho institutions. Legislature-dependent funding renewed annually.

requirements.notes: Must be active drilling eligible non-technician ID National Guard member in good standing. ID institutions only. State fiscal year runs July 1 - June 30.

eligibilityNotes: ID state reimbursement program providing up to $8,000 per state fiscal year (July 1 - June 30) for tuition and fees at institutions inside Idaho. Available to active drilling Idaho Army or Air National Guard members in non-technician status. Legislature-dependent funding - program renewed annually. Can apply 60 days prior to class start through day before classes begin. Can be combined with Chapter 1606 GI Bill (unlike federal TA which cannot). Value: $32,000+ over 4 years. Apply through ID National Guard Education Services Office. Contact via Idaho Military Division website for application procedures and eligibility verification.
